# Trigonometric Functions — Quick Revision Notes

## ⚡ Key Points
- Trigonometric functions are defined for all real numbers.
- Domain of trigonometric functions is all real numbers.
- Range of trigonometric functions is restricted to certain intervals.
- Period of trigonometric functions is the interval after which the function starts repeating.
- Trigonometric functions can be represented in terms of sine and cosine functions.
- Trigonometric functions can be represented in terms of tangent and cotangent functions.
- Period of sine and cosine functions is 2π.
- Period of tangent and cotangent functions is π.
- Graphs of trigonometric functions can be represented in different intervals.
- Trigonometric functions can be expressed in terms of other trigonometric functions using identities.
- Identities are used to simplify trigonometric expressions.
- Trigonometric functions can be used to model real-world problems.
- Sine, cosine, and tangent functions are used to find angles in right-angled triangles.
- Sine, cosine, and tangent functions are used in navigation and geography.
- Trigonometric functions are used in physics and engineering to model periodic phenomena.
- Graphs of trigonometric functions can be used to model real-world situations.
- Trigonometric functions can be used to solve equations and inequalities.
- Trigonometric functions are used in calculus to find derivatives and integrals.
- Trigonometric functions are used in statistics to model data.

## 🔑 Must-Know Definitions
- **Trigonometric Function**: A function that relates the angles of a triangle to the ratios of the lengths of its sides.
- **Domain**: The set of all possible input values (angles) for a function.
- **Range**: The set of all possible output values (values of the function) for a given input.
- **Period**: The interval after which the function starts repeating.
- **Sine Function**: The ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the hypotenuse in a right-angled triangle.
- **Cosine Function**: The ratio of the length of the adjacent side to the length of the hypotenuse in a right-angled triangle.
- **Tangent Function**: The ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the adjacent side in a right-angled triangle.

## 📊 Important Formulas
- **Sine Function Formula**: sin(x) = opposite side / hypotenuse
- **Cosine Function Formula**: cos(x) = adjacent side / hypotenuse
- **Tangent Function Formula**: tan(x) = opposite side / adjacent side
- **Pythagorean Identity**: sin²(x) + cos²(x) = 1
- **Tangent Identity**: tan(x) = sin(x) / cos(x)
- **Cotangent Identity**: cot(x) = cos(x) / sin(x)
- **Periodic Identity**: sin(x + 2π) = sin(x) and cos(x + 2π) = cos(x)
- **Co-Function Identity**: sin(x) = cos(π/2 - x) and cos(x) = sin(π/2 - x)

## ⭐ Exam Tips
- **Understand the definitions and formulas**: Make sure to understand the definitions and formulas of trigonometric functions and their properties.
- **Practice graphing**: Graphing trigonometric functions is an essential skill, practice graphing different functions and identify their periods and ranges.
- **Use identities to simplify expressions**: Use trigonometric identities to simplify complex expressions and solve equations.
- **Practice solving equations and inequalities**: Practice solving equations and inequalities involving trigonometric functions.
- **Use real-world examples**: Use real-world examples to illustrate the application of trigonometric functions in different fields.
